title: Smoky Polish Pork Dumplings
description: A hearty and flavorful Polish-inspired dish featuring tender pork dumplings smothered in a rich smoky sauce. Great for a cold winter evening.
instructions: 2. In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
3. Add the pork shoulder to the bowl and mix until just combined.
4. Divide the dough into 10-12 equal pieces.
5. Roll out each piece into a thin circle.
6. Place a tablespoon of the pork mixture in the center of each circle.
7. Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape, and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
8. In a large skillet, cook the bacon over medium heat until crispy.
9. Remove the bacon from the skillet and set aside.
10. Add the onion and garlic to the skillet and cook until softened.
11. Add the pork dumplings to the skillet and cook until browned on both sides.
12. Transfer the dumplings to a baking dish and top with the bacon.
13. Bake the dumplings in the oven for 20-25 minutes, or until cooked through.
